title: Salesforce batch import truncates phone country codes longer than 2 digits
problem: The nightly batch import from Salesforce into TaskFlow is truncating international phone number country codes to 2 digits. Numbers with 1- or 2-digit country codes (e.g., US +1, UK +44) are imported correctly, but numbers with 3+ digit country codes lose digits — e.g., Ireland's +353 becomes +35, Antigua's +1268 becomes +12. The remainder of each phone number after the country code is preserved correctly. An estimated 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are affected.
root_cause_hypothesis: The Salesforce-to-TaskFlow import logic (or the database column/field storing the country code) is capping the country code portion at 2 characters. This could be a substring/slice operation that hard-codes a length of 2, a database column defined as VARCHAR(2) or CHAR(2), or a regex pattern that only captures up to 2 digits after the '+' sign. Country codes range from 1 to 4 digits per the ITU E.164 standard, so the code needs to handle variable-length codes.
reproduction_steps: In Salesforce, locate or create a contact record with a phone number using a 3+ digit country code (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland)
Wait for the nightly batch import to run (or trigger it manually)
Look up the same contact record in TaskFlow
Observe that the country code is truncated to 2 digits (e.g., +35 1 234 5678)
environment: Production — nightly batch import job pulling from Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow
impact: ~50-100 customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers, causing the support team to reach wrong numbers. Affected customers are in countries with 3+ digit country codes. Data integrity issue that undermines trust in the contact database and blocks support outreach to those customers.
recommended_fix: Inspect the batch import code that parses or stores phone number country codes. Look for hard-coded length limits (substring(0,2), VARCHAR(2), regex capturing only 2 digits after '+'). Update to support variable-length country codes (1-4 digits per E.164). After fixing the import logic, run a one-time re-import or correction pass against Salesforce to repair the ~50-100 already-corrupted records.
proposed_test_case: Create test phone numbers covering 1-digit (+1), 2-digit (+44), 3-digit (+353), and 4-digit (+1268) country codes. Run them through the import pipeline and verify all digits are preserved exactly. Additionally, verify that existing corrupted records are corrected after the remediation import.
information_gaps: Exact name/location of the batch import script or service
Whether this has always been broken or was introduced by a recent change
Whether the truncation happens during parsing, storage, or display
